skill.name: wp-test-analyzer
skill.description: Analyze WordPress theme files and identify forms, links, dynamic content, JavaScript interactions, and WordPress features for E2E test planning.
skill.summary: Extract testable WordPress theme elements for Playwright and E2E test planning.
skill.author: CrazySwami

security_audit.summary: Static findings for shell backticks and weak cryptography are false positives from Markdown formatting strings, documentation code fences, and field names such as description. The analyzer does intentionally read PHP and JavaScript files under a user-supplied theme path, so it should be used only on directories the user intends to inspect.

low_findings.title: Static Shell Execution Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported backtick findings in analyze.py are Markdown inline-code formatting inside generated report strings. SKILL.md contains argument formatting, an example JSON block, and one documented python3 command; no shell execution API is present in the Python code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged Python lines append Markdown strings and do not call subprocess, os.system, eval, or shell APIs. The SKILL.md lines are documentation examples, not executable skill code.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography locations are ordinary description fields and report labels. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or another weak cryptographic operation in those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The matching text is caused by words such as description and output descriptions. There is no cryptographic library import or algorithm invocation near the flagged lines.

low_findings.title: User-Supplied Theme Path Reads Local Files
low_findings.description: The analyzer recursively scans PHP and JavaScript files under the provided theme path and prints extracted test data. This is intended behavior, but users should avoid pointing it at directories containing unrelated private source files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly glob-scans and reads files from the user-supplied path. The behavior is necessary for the analyzer and is limited to local output, with no network exfiltration found.

low_findings.title: Hidden Path Access Match Is Documentation Only
low_findings.description: SKILL.md references a .claude installation path when showing how to run the analyzer. No code reads hidden files because of this documentation path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hidden path appears only in a usage command for the skill's install location. The analyzer reads only the theme path supplied by the user.

dangerous_patterns.title: Recursive Local File Reads From User Path
dangerous_patterns.description: The script uses recursive file discovery and read_text on PHP and JavaScript files below the user-provided theme directory. This is not malicious, but it is a sensitive capability that can expose local source details in generated output.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem access is explicit and central to the skill. No evidence found that the data is sent to a network endpoint or executed as code.

content.user_title: Analyze WordPress Themes for E2E Tests
content.value_statement: WordPress themes often hide test requirements across PHP templates, forms, menus, and JavaScript hooks. This skill extracts those elements so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can plan focused E2E tests.
actual_capabilities: Scans PHP files in a WordPress theme directory.
Extracts forms, methods, actions, fields, nonce fields, and redirect patterns.
Identifies links, page templates, sections, animations, and JavaScript interaction targets.
Detects WP_Query usage, conditionals, post meta reads, custom post types, and theme options.
Outputs analysis as JSON or Markdown for use in E2E test planning.
limitations: It uses regular expressions and may miss complex PHP or generated markup.
It does not run WordPress, load plugins, or inspect database state.
It does not generate complete Playwright tests by itself.
It should only be run against theme directories the user intends to inspect.

use_cases.title: Map Test Coverage for a Theme
use_cases.description: Review templates, forms, links, and dynamic content before writing browser tests.

use_cases.title: Prepare Playwright Scenarios
use_cases.description: Turn extracted forms, redirects, and interaction targets into practical E2E test cases.

use_cases.title: Audit Client Theme Complexity
use_cases.description: Summarize custom post types, theme options, and JavaScript behaviors during project intake.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze a Theme
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner scan of a known theme directory.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the WordPress theme at [theme path] and summarize the forms, templates, links, and dynamic content that need E2E tests.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Test Checklist
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert analyzer output into a QA checklist.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the wp-test-analyzer results for [theme path] to create a prioritized E2E testing checklist for forms, navigation, dynamic content, and JavaScript interactions.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Playwright Coverage
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare implementation guidance for browser tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze [theme path], then propose Playwright test scenarios with selectors, setup notes, and expected assertions based on the detected elements.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Risk Areas
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review for a larger theme.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the analyzer on [theme path] and identify the highest-risk templates for E2E coverage, including forms, redirects, custom post types, and interactive JavaScript behavior.

output_examples.input: Analyze a theme that contains a contact page with required name, email, and message fields.
output_examples.output: The result highlights the contact form, its required fields, its submit method, and nonce information for validation tests.

output_examples.input: Review a portfolio theme with custom post types and animated sections.
output_examples.output: The result lists detected custom post types, section markers, animation functions, and navigation links that should receive browser coverage.

output_examples.input: Prepare tests for a theme with WP_Query loops and option-driven content.
output_examples.output: The result identifies dynamic content patterns and theme options that need seeded content or configuration during test setup.

best_practices: Run the analyzer on a clean local copy of the theme.
Review extracted fields before generating or running E2E tests.
Combine the output with live site behavior checks for dynamic WordPress content.
anti_patterns: Do not point the analyzer at unrelated source trees or home directories.
Do not treat regex results as a complete security or accessibility audit.
Do not generate tests without confirming routes, fixtures, and WordPress configuration.

faq.question: Does this skill execute WordPress code?
faq.answer: No. It reads theme files and extracts patterns from PHP and JavaScript source text.

faq.question: Can it create Playwright tests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides structured analysis for test planning. A separate prompt or workflow is needed to write full tests.

faq.question: What files does it inspect?
faq.answer: It scans PHP and JavaScript files under the WordPress theme path supplied by the user.

faq.question: Is it safe to run on private themes?
faq.answer: Yes, if the user intends to inspect that local theme. The script prints extracted details to local output.

faq.question: Does it detect WordPress plugins?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on theme files and does not analyze plugin directories or database content.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use the results?
faq.answer: The report can guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code when planning WordPress E2E tests.
